title: Mock interview: ML breadth + ML coding round
focus: 6 ML concept questions in 45 min + implement k-means or logreg in 30 min.

tracks.label: Mock · ML breadth + ML coding round

items.label: Breadth round: 6 ML concepts in 45 min
interviewQuestions: Pick 6 ML concept questions and time yourself for 7 min each.
Which concept did you fumble — and what's the cleaner 90-second answer you'd give next time?

items.label: ML coding: implement k-means or logistic regression in 30 min
interviewQuestions: Implement k-means with k-means++ in 30 minutes — and discuss complexity.
What edge cases (empty cluster, all points identical) did you handle, and how?

tracks.label: Startup Practical Loop · Build, debug, and ship under ambiguity

items.label: Ship a working RAG API in 60 minutes: ingestion, retrieval, answer, eval smoke test
interviewQuestions: What is the smallest working RAG API you can build in 60 minutes?
Which shortcuts are acceptable for a demo, and which would create production risk?
What one eval would you add before showing it to a customer?

items.label: Debug a messy notebook with leakage, bad splits, unstable metrics, and unclear product goal
interviewQuestions: What do you inspect first when a take-home notebook reports suspiciously strong performance?
How do you turn a one-off notebook into code another engineer can review and run?
What questions do you ask when the product goal does not map cleanly to the provided labels?

items.label: Week-one execution plan: build-vs-buy, evals, customer feedback, and launch risk
interviewQuestions: What would you do in week one if hired as the first ML engineer at this startup?
How do you choose between improving the model, building evals, cleaning data, or shipping a customer-facing workflow?
What launch risk would you explicitly accept for an MVP, and what risk is non-negotiable?

items.label: AI-assisted coding round: use tools but explain tests, trade-offs, and debugging clearly
interviewQuestions: If LLMs are allowed during the coding exercise, what does the interviewer still need to see from you?
How do you verify generated code for numerical edge cases and performance?
How do you narrate debugging without outsourcing your reasoning to the tool?

references.label: Reddit — practical ML coding questions candidates report
